label: Eurostat German industry wage bill, real annual growth
decision_reason: True monthly wage-bill series; adds a hard observed payroll-cost proxy, but only for German industry.
economic_interpretability: Monthly gross wages and salaries index for German industry, deflated by German HICP annual inflation.
residual_limitation: Sector/geography are narrow; the series measures wage bill, not compensation per employee.

label: European Commission employment expectations indicator
decision_reason: Timely monthly expected-employment pressure proxy from harmonised business surveys.
economic_interpretability: Monthly euro-area employment expectations indicator over the next three months.
residual_limitation: Survey expectations are indirect wage-pressure evidence and can revise with seasonal adjustment.

label: ECB Wage Tracker excluding one-off payments, real
decision_reason: Best monthly compensation-side proxy for persistent real negotiated wage pressure; not actual compensation per employee.
economic_interpretability: Monthly real negotiated wage pressure excluding one-off payments.
residual_limitation: It is negotiated wage pressure, not observed total compensation per employee, payroll income, welfare, or redistribution.

label: European Commission services employment expectations
decision_reason: Monthly services-sector employment expectations add a services wage-pressure timing proxy.
economic_interpretability: Monthly euro-area services employment expectations balance.
residual_limitation: It captures expected services employment, not observed services wages.

label: ECB Wage Tracker headline, real
decision_reason: Monthly negotiated wage signal including smoothed one-off payments; useful robustness around the canonical wage tracker.
economic_interpretability: Monthly real negotiated wage pressure including smoothed one-off payments.
residual_limitation: One-off treatment can mechanically change short-horizon wage dynamics.

label: ECB Wage Tracker with unsmoothed one-off payments, real
decision_reason: Real monthly wage tracker, but one-off payments make it a noisier compensation proxy.
economic_interpretability: Monthly real negotiated wage pressure including unsmoothed one-off payments.
residual_limitation: Noisier one-off payments reduce persistence interpretability.

label: Eurostat unemployment inverse labor-tightness proxy
decision_reason: True monthly labor-tightness proxy; higher transformed value means lower unemployment and tighter labor markets.
economic_interpretability: Negative of the monthly seasonally adjusted euro-area unemployment rate.
residual_limitation: Labor tightness is an indirect wage-pressure proxy, not compensation or wage growth.

label: ECB Wage Tracker excluding one-offs, real 3-month momentum
decision_reason: Momentum captures acceleration in real negotiated wage pressure and is useful for signal-timing checks.
economic_interpretability: Three-month change in real negotiated wage pressure excluding one-off payments.
residual_limitation: Momentum is mechanically noisier than the level and is not used as the primary compensation proxy.

label: ECB Wage Tracker excluding one-offs, real 3-month average
decision_reason: Rolling real wage-pressure proxy smooths month-specific noise without fabricating observations.
economic_interpretability: Three-month average of monthly real negotiated wage pressure excluding one-off payments.
residual_limitation: Smoothing is transparent and only uses observed current/past monthly data; it is not a distinct source series.

label: German retail volume
decision_reason: Monthly real-activity/purchasing-power proxy, not a compensation or wage construct.
residual_limitation: It is not a wage, payroll, labor-income, or compensation construct.

label: ECB Wage Tracker coverage
decision_reason: Coverage is a source-quality diagnostic, not a wage-pressure response variable.
economic_interpretability: Share/coverage diagnostic for the ECB Wage Tracker.
residual_limitation: Useful for revision and representativeness checks only.

label: ECB CES real household income expectations
decision_reason: Monthly household income-pressure expectations are observable, but the public aggregate sample starts late.
economic_interpretability: Weighted median expected household income growth over the next 12 months minus HICP inflation.
residual_limitation: Short public CES sample makes it unsuitable as a canonical long-window compensation proxy.

label: ECB CES expected unemployment rate
decision_reason: Monthly household labor-market expectations are useful as a short-window expectation diagnostic.
economic_interpretability: Weighted median expected unemployment rate over the next 12 months.
residual_limitation: Short sample and expectation construct prevent canonical compensation use.

label: Euro area real compensation per employee, quarter-end observed
decision_reason: Conceptually direct, but observed only at quarter-end; the monthly thesis layer does not interpolate it.
economic_interpretability: Quarterly observed real compensation per employee.
residual_limitation: Sparse quarter-end observations are not a valid monthly compensation proxy without interpolation.
